name: Adhika Cafe
address: 59 Bayswater Rd, Rushcutters Bay NSW 2011, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Finally tried Adhika and it definitely did not disappoint. The place is cozy and nostalgic. It instantly reminded me of my lola’s house, where breakfast always feels comforting and familiar.

I love the creative twists they’ve done on Filipino breakfast classics. Every dish has character and feels thoughtfully put together, not just “instagrammable” but also full of flavour. I had the chilli egg longganisa with bicho, so so delicious, with lots of textures that complemented each other perfectly. The ensaymada was so good, must- try! Love my oat mocha too! Perfect!

You can really feel the passion behind the food. Each plate looks like a piece of art, and the staff and owners are incredibly warm and very friendly. They don’t just serve great food and coffee, they serve it with genuine smiles and conversation. Such a lovely neighbourhood spot.

Second time visiting here and always has the best food and service!!
The sandwich is savory and sweet and full of flavor! The meat has flavor to it self and the pickles definitely add the texture, with the bun super fluffy!!

The ube cake is a must try!! It is pretty dense (not sickening at all) super moisture, not too sweet with natural ube flavor to it since it uses real ube paste without nasties. Definitely going back again!!

Adhika is the kind of place you stumble into once and immediately know you are coming back. We arrived in Sydney needing coffee and ended up finding a true hidden gem. Adhika means “dream or vision,” and this Filipino cafe lives up to the name in every way.

On our first visit I ordered the Chilli Egg Bicho, and bicho… let me tell you, this was the best breakfast I have ever eaten. Not the best on this trip, not the best this year, the best breakfast I have ever had. My friend ordered the Beef Tapa and loved every bite. We both had cappuccinos, and even those were exceptional. The bean matters, and they clearly know what they are doing.

Today was my last day in Australia, and I came back for that same home cooked feeling and familiar warmth. I ordered the Chilli Egg Bicho again and it was just as incredible. The Bicho bread is unreal, soft and chewy and flavorful in a way that makes you stop mid-sentence. The bread is locally sourced about 40 km away from Starlight Bakery in Doonside, a Filipino bakery worth visiting if you are out that way. If you go, get yourself a Bicho. No hesitation.

Today’s service was from Giannina and she was fabulous in every way, warm, attentive, and exactly the kind of person who makes a great meal feel even better.

Long story short and short story long, go. Get some food. Drink the coffee. Enjoy the vibe. Then leave your own review so more people find this beautiful little spot. Adhika is a standout and one of my favorite finds in Sydney.

Really lovely café with delicious food and such friendly service. Everything was fresh, well-seasoned and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, and it feels like the kind of place you could happily linger for a while. I dined in and enjoyed every dish I ordered — great flavours and generous portions. Would definitely come back.

I recently visited Adhika Cafe, and I was honestly impressed.

The sandwich was made with fresh ingredients and had the perfect texture and flavor combination. But the highlight for me was the ube cake soft and full of rich ube flavor.

It’s rare to find a place that does everything this well. Definitely a must-visit 🥰
Specially the staff very friendly and funny.

name: Casa Filipina Bakeshop & Restaurant
address: 54 Oxford Rd, Ingleburn NSW 2565, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We have been a regular customer since they opened their restaurant in Ingleburn. The food is just delicious and reminds me of Philippines.

We ordered their special pancit bihon for friends gathering. Everyone loves it and said the pancit tasted great.

Thank you for your Filipino dishes!

My first time in the shop, got myself the Pandan Shake and some desserts. Never had filipino desserts before, changed my life. The place was nice, the staff were really friendly and enthusiastic and the vibes were great. Definitely coming back to try the other shakes 👍

A True Filipino Gem in Southwest Sydney!

Casa Filipina Bakeshop and Restaurant is an absolute gem in the southwest! It was our first time dining here, and we were celebrating our friends’ 50th birthday—what a blast. From the food to the service to the atmosphere, everything was spot on.

They served
pansit malabon (palabok), crispy pata kare-kare, sisig, fresh lumpia, pork BBQ, lumpia shanghai, and fried chicken—each dish tasted authentically Filipino and clearly prepared with care.

I was fortunate to be seated next to Oly, one of the owners, who shared amazing stories about how the restaurant started. He even explained how they prepare their dishes the traditional way—especially my favourites, the sizzling sisig and dinakdakan. To top it all off, he personally prepared brewed coffee for us during dessert!

My wife and daughter also asked me to buy from their bakeshop—sapin-sapin, yema cake, and leche flan—and all of them tasted awesomely Filipino.

I hate to compare, but here we go: Casa Filipina beats the usual places we go to around Blacktown—Mama Lor, Sir Manong’s, and the A-Team. I truly hope they expand and open a branch in the northwest of Sydney!

Highly recommended. Will definitely be back!

This was my first time trying Filipino food, so I don't have anything to compare it to. However, the meat was quite flavorful, though slightly on the dry side. Some items at the counter had names but no descriptions, which made me hesitant to try them. That said, I’d definitely return to explore more items on the menu.

Not only was the rice tasty, the tocino deliciously sweet, and the egg just perfect... I watched the staff assist an elderly customer with extreme patience and care. Well done. Also, it's good that they didn't mind my mixed up TagCano. I was trying to exercise use of Tagalog and Ilocano and it seemed to be good enough here. I feel encouraged now. Thank you. My wife and I love this place!

name: Charcoal & Crisp Lechon Sydney
address: 16 Falcon St, Crows Nest NSW 2065, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
CHARCOAL AND CRISP IN CROWS NEST

If you’re craving authentic Filipino food in Sydney, Charcoal and Crisp Lechon in Crows Nest is an absolute must-visit. Everything we tried was bursting with flavor and nostalgia, but the lechon was the undisputed star of the show — the skin impossibly crispy and flavorful, the meat juicy and tender.

Another standout was the lechon sisig, served sizzling with an egg on top. Perfectly seasoned, rich, and comforting — it’s the kind of dish that makes you want to come back for more.

We also loved the watermelon sinigang with prawns — a playful twist on a classic, both refreshing and tangy — as well as the fried pompano and ensaladang talong, which balanced the richness of the meats beautifully.

And don’t skip dessert. The ube cake here is divine: light, moist, not overly sweet, and packed with that signature purple yam flavor Filipinos know and love.

The venue itself is warm and inviting, with a casual yet vibrant ambiance that makes you feel right at home. If you’re a Filipino food lover — or just curious about discovering new flavors — Charcoal and Crisp in Crows Nest deserves a spot at the top of your list.

(3.5 stars)
Fat rolls of pork belly are on display on the kitchen pass at Charcoal & Crisp Lechon. The namesake golden-skinned, spit-roasted pork—lechon—is the focus of the short menu at this Crows Nest newcomer. The pork is cooked Manila-style, in a simple blend of salt, pepper and spice, then chopped and served with chips (or rice) and salad ($22.50). The fatty richness is balanced by a vinegar-heavy dipping sauce that employs the pig’s liver. Naturally sweet watermelon juice ($8) helps your palate recover from the salt.

And you will need to recover, especially if you also hit up the lechon sisig ($19.50). Here the chopped pork belly is turned onto a sizzling, cast-iron plate with mild green chilli, onion, and a raw egg that sets right before your eyes. Even a liberal squeeze of calamansi—the favoured citrus of Pinoy people—isn’t enough to save you from a powerful thirst. I suggest combining it with ensaladang talong ($15) and using the grilled eggplant salad’s fresh tomatoes and washed Spanish onion to further calm things down.

The pork also makes its way into lechon kare-kare ($19.50) in crisp cubes and big sheets of crackling. With the bagoong (sautéed shrimp paste) passed separately, I found this peanut-based curry a little bland and disappointing, though my salt-drenched palate appreciated the inclusion of juicy green beans and bok choy. Perhaps I just favour the Cebu-style version I first had in Enmore, where lemongrass and a metric tonne of garlic were stuffed inside the roast meat, and the kare-kare was balanced with bagoong by the kitchen. To make a booking here you have to pay a ten buck deposit. It’s refundable against whatever you eat, or the cost of bringing a bottle of wine (think: acidity) to enjoy with your eats.

The best lechon I’ve had in Sydney. The skin is super crispy and the meat is really tasty. The ensaladang talong was really good, especially the bagoong. The staff are very friendly, the price is fair, and the portions are worth it. I’ll definitely be coming back!! ❤️👌

We had such a great experience here! The restaurant serves amazing Filipino food — the lechon is so crispy and tasty, the pompano is delicious, and the prawn watermelon sinigang is a must-try, packed with fresh prawns and heaps of veggies. We also ordered the Feast for 2, and it was definitely worth it.

Not to mention, the ube dessert was really good too — not overly sweet, but you can really taste the ube halaya.

A big shout-out to Hazel as well — she was super accommodating and provided excellent customer service, which made our visit even better. I’d happily rate this place a solid 4.9/5, leaving just a little room for even more improvement. Highly recommended!

The Lechon is good but the Lumpiang Shanghai, Sisig, Kare Kare, and Ube cake are our go to dishes.
Hoping to one day wash all this down with a beer but don’t let that put you off, they sell out of food for a reason.

Consulate General of the Philippines
address: Unit 1824/33 Wentworth Ave, Sydney NSW 2000, Australia
description: A verified Filipino community in Sydney.
reviews:
I would like to express my sincere appreciation to the Philippine Consulate for the excellent service we received during my child’s passport renewal.
Special mention to Rowena (Weng) for being incredibly helpful, professional, and accommodating.🥰 She guided us through the entire process and even assisted me with photocopying the documents😊 which I truly appreciated. Everything was smooth and stress-free. The processing was amazingly fast, it only took about 15 minutes and we were completely dond😇
Thank you for your efficiency and dedication to serving the Filipino community. Your outstanding service is truly commendable and very much appreciated.😇🤩

Extremely disappointing experience at the Philippine Consulate General in Sydney.

You would think that an office representing the Philippines overseas would at least have clear instructions and professional staff. Apparently that is too much to expect.

The website provides almost no useful guidance. Important information like cut off times, required mailing envelopes, correct forms to download, and other requirements are either missing or unclear. So people show up thinking they prepared everything, only to be told they downloaded the wrong forms or are missing something that was never even mentioned online. It feels less like a government service and more like a guessing game.

And then there is the customer service.

One bald, dark skinned male staff member at the counter was incredibly sarcastic and dismissive when answering questions. Instead of helping applicants understand the process, he seemed more interested in making sarcastic remarks and acting irritated that people were asking for clarification.

There was also an older lady staff member who behaved in a similarly rude and condescending way. Rather than guiding people, the tone was impatient and unfriendly, as if applicants were bothering them simply for being there.

This is a consulate. People come here for official documents and services. They should not have to deal with attitude, sarcasm, or staff who clearly do not want to assist the public.

Between the lack of transparency, the confusing instructions, the surprise requirements like envelopes and photocopies, and the extremely unprofessional behaviour from staff, the whole experience leaves a terrible impression.

If this office represents the Philippines abroad, then the standards need serious improvement. Professionalism, transparency, and basic respect should not be this difficult to provide.

Worst customer service experience I have had in a long time.

I had a truly wonderful experience at the Consulate General of the Philippines in Sydney. The service was very pleasant, welcoming, and efficient. Everything was completed quickly and completely stress-free. All the staff were friendly, professional, and accommodating. Mr. Many Galvez kindly helped me organise everything I needed for my Philippine passport renewal, making the entire process smooth and easy. Ms. Rowena Maria was also very supportive and helpful. They are genuinely amazing staff, and I am very grateful for their assistance. Overall, I am extremely satisfied with the service and highly appreciate their excellent work. 😊

The male staff were quite rude and are very passive aggressive. This makes it very difficult to interact with them. One staff member gave up on taking my photo and said that if it gets rejected that I’d have to pay for it instead when they’re supposed to make ways. We pay for the service to WORK not for us to come back because it didnt work the first time. Do better

I recently processed my SPAs, report of birth, and dual citizenship application, and I couldn't be more pleased with the service I received. The staff were incredibly helpful and respectful throughout the entire process. They were very responsive to my messages and even took the initiative to call me with updates on my application. Their attention to detail and commitment to customer service truly stood out. Thank you Ma'am and Sir ❤️

name: Cucina de Manila
address: 28/30 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
This restaurant is a must-try for Filipino dishes. We ordered and tasted almost everything on the menu but everything is cooked so perfectly, that it made it hard for us to choose which dish is the top 1😂🙈

But for me, the best of bestest dish of all is their Crispy Pata. I’ve already tasted this almost 3 times when I stayed 3 weeks in Sydney. And as a Filipina traveller, I suggest going to this place, because it’s rare to find food here in Sydney that is worth your money and will satisfy your cravings. Crispy Pata is also the best food for gatherings and celebrations with family and friends. And, by the way, this resto is near the Featherdale Wildlife Park, we went here again after our fun and tiring walk in the Wildlife Park.

We also tried The Crispy Dinuguan, we ordered this because the “crispy” intrigued us, and it did not disappoint. Another best one from their menu, is the Grilled Pork Belly, sooooo tasty that it would make you order it again for dinner.

And, as a Bicolana, Laing is a must-try too, it’s so fulfilling and tasty. Pork Sisig is sooooooo yummy also, the sisig plate is full of meat and flavor.

As for their Kare-Kare, Bangus, Pinakbet, and Palabok, I don’t usually order these in the Philippines, because they did not taste good, but I never imagined that I would like their cooking for these dishes, especially being able to taste them in AU, since it’s hard to create a tasty flavor for these dishes. Dabest! Will surely come back again when we travel back to Sydney. Oh, and their Sagu’t Gulaman and Calamansi beverages are a must-drink also, especially in the summer heat of AU.

Overall, their dishes made us Filipinos feel like we’re “home away from home”. To all Filipino and Filipino food-lovers out there, support and try out this resto. It’s so worth it! The staff is very accommodating and helpful, just a tip also, they’re sometimes booked with a lot of orders, dine-in, and boodle fight bookings, so please be patient 💖😋😋✨

This the best Filipino food in Sydney indeed! After our tour in Featherdale zoo, we head directly to this restaurant as it is the closest we can find.

Their Crispy Pata is crispy on the outside, tender in the inside. We also ordered Kare-Kare, Pinakbet, Dinuguan. All of them are perfectly cooked.

The staffs are very welcoming and attentive. Will definitely go back for more!

Ordered palabok xl tray and crispy pata for Christmas. We have had crispy pata before and it was always perfect and the best anywhere we’ve been but this time it was overcooked and was so dry and tough. The palabok was recommended by a friend but unfortunately, it didn’t have enough sauce for the xl tray, the toppings were just eggs, lemon and spring onions - no prawns, tofu, maybe just a bit of mince (I’m not even sure). My sister in law had to cook extra sauce or else it will be just wasted. I’m not sure if they changed chef or owners but definitely no one in our party will order from them.

Classic Filipino dishes on the menu and everything is tasty. This is my fave crispy pata in Sydney, hands down. Food delicious. Service fast and friendly. Space is clean and walls are decorated with Manila scenes.

The only downside is the facility - need to improve toilet facilities in Filipino restaurants.

Food is okay.
Prices are above the normal price.
When we arrived, some dishes on thr menu were not available. The tissues are limited. Had to ask for it.
The Ube shake did not taste like Ube it taste more like vanilla. I was very disappointed. No halo halo available.
Any soup were big on liquid, not many vegetables or meat. You will be swimming with soup. Great for people who loves soup. Bring your rice with you. Expensive to buy rice too.
My experience is good but I was expecting better.

Descanso
address: Shop 4/32-72 Alice St, Newtown NSW 2042, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Tried Descanso for brunch today and we were very impressed.  A clean and tidy cafe with a great location a little bit away from the madness of King St in Newtown giving plenty of options for free street parking in the surrounding area (1 or 2 hour limit).  Fantastic menu with a range of “Aussie” food options for the non-adventurous and a delicious range of authentic Filipino breakfast and lunch selections.  Some menu options are only available on the weekend.  Service was friendly and fast while the food was absolutely delicious accompanied by fresh (bottled) juices and great coffee.  Cheesy Pandesal was a scrumptious accompaniment to our meal too.  We grabbed some extra dishes to take home with us but I’m sure we will be back again sometime soon to work our way through the menu. Highly recommended!

We really enjoyed our visit to Descanso Café 🥰
The owners, ate Regine & Kuya Wilbur are so kind and accommodating. You can really feel the heart they put into the café and how much they care about their customers ❤️
They have so many yummy Filipino breakfast dishes and filo favourites! Come check it out ✨

As someone who has been to many Filipino restaurants — and being Filipino myself who can cook these dishes at home — I’m always on the lookout for a good Filipino restaurant for those days when I’m feeling lazy to cook but still craving comfort food.

This place came highly recommended by a friend, so we were excited to give it a try. The restaurant itself looks nice, and the food presentation was on point. However, sadly, the experience was quite underwhelming, especially for the price point.

We ordered the Barkada Share Board ($52), which was advertised as good for 2–3 people. Realistically, it barely served even two. At that price, I was expecting a hearty Filipino feast with perhaps even leftovers to take home — but that wasn’t the case at all. The longganisa was disappointingly tiny (about the size of my thumb), the garlic rice lacked any real garlic flavour (which defeats the purpose of calling it garlic rice), and the portions across the board felt stingy.

We also tried the Turon for dessert. For $8, we received just two small pieces cut in half to make it look like four. Honestly, this felt like a total rip-off.

After dining, I decided to check the reviews online and noticed a concerning pattern. While the owners do respond to feedback, the tone comes across as defensive and passive-aggressive. Instead of taking on board constructive criticism, they seem to blame or argue with the customers who leave honest reviews. This kind of attitude is disappointing and shows a lack of willingness to improve the dining experience.

I wanted to love this place. The vibe and presentation had potential, but sadly, the food quality, portion size, and pricing left me feeling very dissatisfied. There are so many other Filipino restaurants in Sydney offering more authentic flavours, better portions, and respectful customer engagement.

Would I return? Probably not. Filipino food is meant to be hearty, generous, and comforting — unfortunately, this was none of that.

Tried my first Filipino restaurant in Sydney and it was a great experience! The food was delicious — I had the beef pares, which was a bit on the salty side for my taste, but a squeeze of lemon balanced it out perfectly. The flavors were rich and comforting. Service was top-notch, friendly, and attentive — couldn’t ask for better. Definito worth a visit if you’re curious about Filipino cuisine!

Love, love, love DESCANSO!! ❤️ My family and I have been enjoying their food for years - way before they moved from Glebe to Newtown. We keep coming back not just because of their delicious food… but also because of the lovely Filipinos (husband & wife team) who own and run this Cafe Restaurant - together with their awesome team.

It’s definitely one of the best places to eat at here in Sydney - especially if you want to taste some delicious, authentic Filipino cuisine. I almost forgot to mention - their lechon and sisig are “must have” dishes when you come to visit.

Make sure to try all their popular mains PLUS the delicious deserts. We love the various “***silog” dishes, the yummy adobo, kare-kare, sinigang, palabok, Filipino spaghetti and MORE! 😋 My basic photos can’t properly describe how good their food are - you must come and taste them yourself.

See you again soon, DESCANSO family! Keep up the good work. 🙌🏽❤️

address: 14/18 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Its not very often and very reluctant to eat in a filipino restaurant when I'm here at home in Sydney but I thought I'd give this place a try. Its our 2nd time eating here and feel comfortable coming back again and again. Located opposite Blacktown station just down the Main Street escalators. Service is great with friendly staff. Plenty of seating. Freshly cooked food is delicious plus its affordable with 2 choices and a serve of hot white rice for $15. Additional rice is $3 - 3.50. They'll have your favourite pinoy ulams like afritada, adobo, dinuguan, bicol express, kaldereta and more. And my favourite desert halo-halo at $10.
Food is a bit bland. Serving size is decent. If you're craving Filipino food, you might consider other restaurants around the area.

discovered this hidden gem today.. great for those pinoys who are craving for some home cooked food. the place is comfortable with a functional ac to cool off from the blasting summer heat. the set up is a modern carenderia with lots of condiments (one pinoy trademark).

breakfast at 10 dollars. regular meal starts at 10-12 dollars. soft drinks is sold at 2 for cans and 3 of 390 ml bottles.

pinoy dessert such as halo halo is sold at 8. Turon at 2. many more options.

definitely will be coming back.

Recently, I went to the same shop for 2-3 times. the quality of food isn’t consistent as compared to the first time I came and tried the food. I took food home and noticed that the fried fish was fully soaked with cooking oil thus making it soggy. I think they can do better. Best to go early than go there when they are about to close - food not warm and the taste isn’t as good when they are freshly cooked.
Had topsilog at $15 with garlic rice, egg, vege and pork. Very delicious. Service wasn't too great but didn't have issues unlike the other comments.

ordered tapa with egg and garlic rice for $15. Worst garlic fried rice ive ever had, it was hard. I ended up paying another $3 for half plain rice.

Ambiance is good.

reviews:
Serving size is a bit small for the price. Plates are an extra 20c.

The sisig was the only dish I enjoyed. Though it can be a bit oily for some, the flavour is good. Good thing ends there. Kaldereta flavour was good, but the beef was a bit tough. Garlic rice should be renamed to butter rice with garlic bits on top and not even lukewarm. The bbq (chicken and pork) were cold and tough as well.

Service was just normal.

$12 BBQ pork skewer with garlic rice and coconut water. Great value for the portion. The pork was fatty in the best way, juicy, flavorful, and still thick and meaty.

Easily some of the best Filipino BBQ I’ve had.

The spot is in a busy food court near the escalators and elevators, so there is always a lot of foot traffic, but that is just the nature of the location.

Definitely my go to whenever I am in the area.
I wanted Filipino food and Filo Lasa in Blacktown hit the spot. The food gave me a taste of home. I highly recommend the turon!

Most take away shops fill up the containers that the food spills. At Filo Lasa, the container is only 80% filled. When I brought it up, the server defensively responded by saying that they weighed the food. And that it should be 400 grams. The serving is also poor even when you dine in.

Great food but measly quantity. Ladies were giving Caucasian customers large quantities and friendly smiles but gave me smaller portions and a cold attitude. Maybe ill bring a white friend next time lol. Staff will treat me like a King.

Response: thanks for acknowledging but your staff needs to treat customers equally. I've been here more than 5 times now and never had a positive interaction with your staff. They're cold and look annoyed to serve you but yesterday was different when they were very happy to serve others but when I stepped in the same rude attitude came across. As I said, I love the food but it's pushing customers like me away.

reviews: For all your Filipino snacks, spices, food and sweet cravings,  visit Filo Pantry. They also have the cheapest but quality coffee and hot choco drinks. Shop is only 200 meters from Merrylands Station.
You feel right at home with the friendly service and a great selection of products that give you a little boost if you’re feeling a little home sick.
Thanks boys great service
if you cook and appreciate good ingredients, I love this place

name: Filo Pride Asian Grocery, Eatery and Catering
address: 211 Marrickville Rd, Marrickville NSW 2204, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I have tried several eateries/cafes around Sydney, Lidcombe, Kogarah, Rockdale, Newtown. This place by far is my favourite! Food is pretty much on point. I tried the Dinuguan and Palabok, I was not disappointed it took me back to my roots and felt so happy. The place was neat and tidy with many Filipino groceries on offer and their customer service was excellent. We will definitely be coming back. Salamat Po!

We loved all their Filipino dishes when we went for the buffet. For $25/person, it was very much worth it. The flavours were spot on and transports you back to the Philippines with their authentic Filipino traditional flavours. We also like shopping for basic Filipino pantry items. Everyone from their team is very friendly and customer service was exceptional. Will definitely be back and highly recommend this shop.

The staff are friendly, accommodating and always have a welcoming smile to greet you as you enter their establishment.  They showcase authentic delicious Filipino cuisine which you have the option to eat all you can style.  Their per dish meal is also available if you are not that hungry.  Their wide variety of Filipino desserts are available as well.  They also have the basic Filipino ingredients they sell if you want to cook your own food or restock your Filipino pantry.  This is an all in one stop to satisfy your Filipino cravings.

Great place to get my filo essentials. The lady serving me was so lovely & friendly.

There were a few filo ladies talking on the high tables having a chat with the staff which I thought was nice touch.

There is a wide selection of takeaway food options which I’ll hopefully get to try next time if I haven’t already eaten lunch.

Good selection of snacks, desserts & filo pantry essentials.

I visited this Filipino grocery in Marrickville during Marrickville Music festival. They sell hot foods and on the weekends & they also sell Halo Halo! I tried the pork & chicken skewers and they were tasty. I’m keen to try their other foods next time!

description: A verified Filipino church in Sydney.
reviews: My church that I attend, a Filipino Adventist community that ranges from babies to older persons. Every last Saturday of the month is usually closed, but otherwise we meet every Saturday. We usually have main service first at 930am for approx 1hr, 5min break then Sabbath school at 1130am-1230pm, then potluck lunch. We have an Adventurers Club, Pathfinders Club, active youth team, passionate leadership team, and friendly members. Please send a message to us if you have any questions, prayer requests or any other needs that we can help meet. We hope to connect to other Filipinos in the community, and anyone and everyone are welcome. Main service is in English, and Sabbath school classes are all English except 2 adult classes- Tagalog and Bisaya.
Youthful and active church. Excellent potlucks!

I love doing my grocery shopping here. Everything is so cheap compared to Coles/woolies. Fruits and veggies are really cheap and fresh. Their meat section is really good too. I only shop here maybe once a month and buy a lot. My only issue is they don’t have trolleys that you can take to your car so you pretty much have to carry everything. don't buy the bulalo meat on the meat area paid $43.91 and this bones weighs like a kilo so i paid $15.99 for this bones! 😑

description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: A Filo inspired menu that is modern take of favourite Pinoy  dishes. We started with the Adobo spring rolls with the vinegarette/ketchup sauce on the side as the usual condiment (sawsawan). We then had Nasi Goreng in brown rice and a full bowl of Batangas Lomi. We are happy customers and will be back again. Would love to see more protein dishes in the menu like chicken and fish.

Greenknowe Café is a charming, Filo‑inspired brunch spot known for its excellent coffee, creative menu, and genuinely welcoming service. The atmosphere is cosy yet lively, with both indoor seating and a pleasant outdoor terrace that locals love. A café with personality, great flavours, and a neighbourhood feel

Neat little cafe tucked away in Potts Point. Was very busy when we arrived , but food and drinks still arrived in a timely manner. Great variety of food from bacon and egg rolls to filipino comfort food. Make sure to ask for any specials for the day ! 10/10

Best little Filipino fusion cafe tucked away on Greenknowe Ave! The pork belly in the Ultimate Tocino was perfectly cooked and I’ll pay Phil’s (Ube) Pancakes the ultimate Asian compliment: “not too sweet!” I cannot wait to come back and try their other delectables. 🤤

Ordered the Tocino and Beef Tapa and it was absolutely wonderful! Coffee was great too! Great cafe that offer a cozy environment to sit back and relax. Most importantly the service and people are really great and friendly . Will definitely come back again!

reviews: Seriously! A hidden gem don't tell anyone .... comy cushions on wicker seats ..  so clean and nice..  served in a cup and saucer with a smile ... fantastic coffee👍👍😁😁😁

Thank you Halo Halo Cafe for the generous gift. Such a lovely, yummy, perfect birthday treat! Lucky to have a great cafe close-by where we’re always treated like family. 🤩 we always look forward to coming back because of the yummy food and great service!

Such a good place to eat! My family and friends love going here anytime we have a celebration for their gorgeous brunch options. I also work next to the cafe and their takeaway options are fantastic for my work lunch breaks xx

Halo Halo Café, the cozy, beautifully decorated space creates a welcoming ambiance. Their coffee is rich and creamy, used The Grounds Coffee Roastery. The friendly staff go above and beyond to ensure a delightful experience. Whether you’re looking for a peaceful spot to relax or catch up with friends, this café is the perfect choice. Highly recommended for its great atmosphere, fantastic service, and flavorful offerings!

Very homely and friendly service, food was also delicious. They are also inspired by Vietnamese food, explaining why banh mi is on the menu. The barbecue pork banh mi was flavourful and filling. The adobo loco moco is delicious, though rice was slightly greasy.
Halo halo is one of my favourite desserts and they make it very well here, definitely justified ordering it for our brunch.
Good coffee and the mug size is huge!

Hi Dins kitchen
address: 226 Queen St, St Marys NSW 2760, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Hi Dins is our go to for Filipino Desserts and Empanadas. Their classic pulled pork empanada, creamy chicken and sundried beef will not disappoint. The golden, flaky crust had just the right amount of crunch, giving way to a savory, well-seasoned filling.

Can't fault this place - price on food is unbeatable!! The setting was nice and clean and it doesn't feel like you're just walking in another cafe/eatery - it feels welcoming and cosy. Despite the lack of parking due to a busy major street at St. Mary's, trust and believe the food will more than make up for the walk!!
Had the chance to try out a few of their dishes including the tapsilog, tocilog, lechon paksiw and pork emapmadas (the best in the west, I promise!!) and needless to say, it was beyond my expectations! (and they were high!) The flavours were spot on, nothing too salty or bland - it was perfect! I need not to say anymore - if my exclamation marks weren't enough to dress this place with praise then you're on own and YOURE MISSING OUT! *mic drop*

Cannot believe I haven't left a review yet as I have been ordering from here for years. The food is always fantastic, the prices are extremely fair and affordable. My family always asks for Empanadas when we host events.

The owners are extremely nice and accommodating. It is clear they are passionate and give great care and attention to their products.

If you come here you will not regret it.

I bought traditional pork and chicken empanadas in this place today and can definitely say that they are amazing! I brought a box of 20 to a party and everyone absolutely loved it. Would definitely recommend!
Pulled pork empanadas with quail egg was a crowd pleaser! Very warm accomodating service.

name: Inigo's Cafe & Grill
address: 22 Rooty Hill Rd S, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We’re so happy that Inigo opened a branch here in the West, closer to our home. My husband and I dined there yesterday and were extremely satisfied with the food, service, and the clean, elegant ambiance.
We ordered the pork barbecue skewers, crispy beef kare-kare, and crispy dinuguan. Everything was delicious, beautifully presented, and truly went beyond our expectations.
It’s wonderful to support a Filipino family-owned and managed restaurant serving authentic Pinoy recipes with a unique twist. The young gentleman at the counter, who served our food and made the coffee we ordered, was very accommodating, polite, and professional.
I’ve also tried their sisig at the Bexley branch, and it’s one of the best sisig dishes I’ve tasted in Australia.
We will surely come back to try other menu items. Highly recommended to our Filipino friends and to other cultures who would love to experience authentic Filipino cuisine!
Every meal came out from the kitchen was absolutely delicious! Couldn’t fault any of it! Lovely chill vibes. And service is great! 100% recommend it for people to try! ❤️😊

My husband and I went around the afternoon. The resto ambiance and decor was clean and simple. So my expectations for the food was good or above. We ordered the following;
Sisig- salty and a let down. We didn’t finished it even my husband who loves sisig one taste only.
Grilled na liempo- disappointed just looking at it ketchup marination.
Pansit guisado- very oily and salty. I think the soy sauce they used is dark soy sauce.
Bulalo - i was going to order this because i only wanted to have a hot soup. Other filo resto gives out hot soup.But they told me they don’t give out sabaw so i ordered the bulalo. Looks ok but the after taste is bitter and burn. We found burn pieces of meat in the soup.
Inihaw na pusit- maybe this one is the one that we finished even though its not smoky. The squid was soft and not salty at all. That’s why i gave 2 stars on the review otherwise only 1 star overall.
Garlic rice- its ok.

Service- the lady in the cashier was cheerful and friendly. I think she’s a bit overwhelmed being all around cashier, server, table We don’t mind waiting as we know filo food takes time to cook.

Overall we went there wanted to try it and if its good we will definitely come back. But the food was a let down. I wanted to come back and support pinoy so i can recommend it to other pinoy but i find the food lacking and very salty. Will not come back.

Would have never known about this place if it wasn’t for EatClub. The atmosphere was unfortunately very loud because of the music, you could hardly talk to each other and enjoy a conversation, which made the dining experience less enjoyable.

The food was overall okay for Filipino comfort food. We ordered the sisig taco, chicken inasal rice plate, lechon kawali rice plate, and mango sago for dessert.

The sisig taco was good, though a bit heavy, but had very good flavours.

The chicken inasal rice plate was great and had adequate, well balanced flavours.

The lechon kawali, however, was a big disappointment. It was extremely dry and very oily, with more fat than meat and crispy skin. I understand lechon kawali can sometimes be dry since it’s pan or deep-fried, but this one was so dry and hard it almost felt like it could break a tooth. The amount of vinegar sauce I had to use on my rice just to eat the dish to cut out the fatty/oil taste.

For dessert, the mango sago was much thicker than I prefer. Generally I am used to a bit more runny mango sago which has more flavour but the thickness made it less appealing to eat more of a pudding based.

The servers though were welcoming and friendly though. But it took the awhile to acknowledge us and sat us to a table.

Overall, it was average Filipino comfort food, which was a bit disappointing.

We had such a great experience at this Filipino café! We ordered the Fairy Halo-Halo, Ube Latte, Lechon Kawali, fried rice, and lumpia — and everything was absolutely delicious. The Fairy Halo-Halo had a modern twist but still tasted so homey and comforting. The Ube Latte is my new favourite coffee order. The Lechon Kawali was perfectly crispy and flavorful, and the lumpia and fried rice were just as amazing.
The café itself is very neat and beautifully modern, with subtle hints of Filipino-inspired design that add so much character to the space. I loved how it felt both stylish and culturally warm at the same time. You can really tell they put thought into both the food and the atmosphere.
Highly recommend if you’re craving authentic Filipino flavors in a modern yet homey setting!

description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.

Kanto Bagnet
address: 120 Crown St, Riverstone NSW 2765, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
If you’re near Riverstone and looking for great Filipino food, Kanto Bagnet is definitely worth a visit. It’s a food truck setup but they also have tables and chairs so you can dine in.

Their food tastes amazing, even the garlic rice, dips, and sauces are very flavorful. Our favorite is the Sisig Bagnet - crispy and tasty! Their crispy pork belly and pork BBQ come with a delicious Aristocrat-style sauce that makes every bite better. The chicken inasal is also very tasty, and you can drizzle their chicken oil over your rice if you want that “Mang Inasal” style experience.

They also offer weekly specials, so it’s good to ask what’s available. For snacks. we love their kwek-kwek and chicharon bulaklak, and their bulalo soup is rich and full of flavor. We are yet to try their desserts.

The owner and staff are very friendly, and they even have a filtered water machine for customers. We enjoy eating here so much!

Tip: Check their business hours on Google before visiting. To make sure your favorite dishes are still available, it’s also a good idea to give them a quick call ahead.

Tried Kanto Bagnet Sydney and really enjoyed the food. We ordered the bagnet sisig, java rice, laing, and pork belly BBQ. Everything was tasty and well cooked. The bagnet sisig was crispy and full of flavour, the laing was creamy with a nice bit of spice, and the pork belly BBQ was juicy and nicely grilled.

The java rice paired really well with everything. Overall, a solid spot if you’re craving Filipino food. Would definitely come back.

Kanto Bagnet has got to be my new favourite place. Every week I look through their specials and their “Veggie Friday”. Hindi nakakasawa. Lumpiang Shanghai, Munggo, Laing, Ampalaya with Bagnet, Palabok, BBQ..each bite takes me back home. Today they accidentally gave me the grilled liempo instead of the Laing (the liempo was good btw) but as soon as they realised their mistake, they straight away offered to deliver the Laing (and omg ang sarap ng Laing!). The best. Thank you Kanto Bagnet team for making our tummies happy. 😊
Prob the best Bagnet and Sisig i’ve had in Sydney. Must try! Truly tastes like home!
The food is fresh and delicious! They have large servings too!☺️❤️

Always nice to see a filipino eatery. Not many of them around & wished there were.  Food was tasty they had a good variety to choose from & same goes for the grocery. Good service.  Keep up the good work guys 😃

After recerching the place on FB , went for lunch with hubby worst ever food we have eaten in a philipino eatery.The man in charge was rude to the casher..?? Food was  rely terrible , poor quality and expensive for what you get   Whow how can people recommend the place????, Never again

Local Filipino food and grocery shop..
Family style cooking.

$15 rice + 1 dish

Boodle fight food is well prepared. Staff-- they are accommodating. We held our baby shower and they even allowed us put some decor and conduct simple program. 👍👍👍

Extensive menu and friendly customer service!
We visited to try something different in the area. We were pleased to find such a comprehensive menu in comparison to most Filipino restaurants in Sydney.
The waiter was very friendly and helpful with explaining each dish to us.
Our fish and meat dishes were cooked perfectly.
We definitely recommend giving it a try as we'll be back to try more of the menu!

We usually order on uber eats and the food is consistently good. I really enjoy their spaghetti - brings back memories of my childhood. It's also not overly sweet. But today, we decided to dine in and what an experience that was! They have a karaoke machine with thousands (I think. There were a lot) of songs, which made lunch super fun. Definitely a winning combination. And the staff are nice and friendly. Love this place.

Their food taste good, ordered 3 dishes for catering which taste great and made on time.
On the same day picking up the food I didnt feel welcomed. The ladies at the front didnt greet me, I had to apporach them myself because one of them was glued to their phone. I had to ask for a certain dessert 3 times for the lady to pay attention, which ended being expired with mold on it.

name: Kasalo Resto Grill Granville
address: 44 Railway Parade, Granville NSW 2194, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I just bought a pork curry from this place, and what I witnessed right after paying was shocking. Flies were swarming over the raw pork meat and even on the covered meals. After checking other reviews, I realized this isn’t an isolated incident—many customers have reported the same issue, and it’s absolutely true. This is unacceptable and a serious health hazard. This establishment should be reported to the health authorities immediately.

I ordered in Kasalo through Uber Eats and the food we received (sisigsilog) has a big fly insect cooked with the fried egg! I called the restaurant directly and no apologies were given. The lady on the line just told me that she will relay it to their chef and owner.

This is a food safety issue and the food safety authority should investigate this. I also reported it to Uber Eats and is waiting for their reply and refund.

This is not the first time as my daughter bought takeaway food and the rice is spoiled - so beware and do not buy food from this restaurant anymore!

I am proud to take my friends here to experience real Filipino dishes. Love the atmosphere (Native Kubo style), very casual yet clean and presentable. Food tasty and not too pricey and if you really love the food and pinoy merchandised there is a Pinoy store next or attached to it for Take away food and little groceries...

Friday and Saturday from 600pm is normally full house due ACCOUSTIC Live performances aside from daily Karaoke. Being an intimate and small dining area BOOKING is a required.

ENJOY Pinoy Food and Entertainment.

We went to eat the shop - good value for money - rice combo and 2 dishes including a drink and all for 40 bucks - servings / portions were big too thank u - will visit again ! FYI as compared to other commercial filo restos this place gives you a feel of a canderina from PH

This is not the first time i ordered from Kasalo through Uber Eats. My previous orders the food were ok. But this time, the Pancit canton and Chicharon bulaklak was very salty. My 4 years old could't eat the Pancit and its his favourite.

address: 80 Blacktown Rd, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We hit up Kowboy Grill the other night with our friends, and we had an awesome time.  Kowboy Grill Blacktown is now under new management, and wow, there’s been such a huge improvement since we last went. The food was served fresh and was delicious, along with the live band. The M7 Band were great performers, and they got the people up and dancing to Filipino hits and classic tunes from the 80s and 90s.

Kowboy Grill also offers a great selection of drinks, beers, and cocktails.  - We got some buckets of San Mig and Red Horse - the vibe really takes you back to the Philippines.  Even my Gin & Tonic was very Pinoy, they were serving up Ginebra San Miguel Gin  - Kaya mas may Amats! LOL even the Gin Pamelo!

Best of all, the staff were all super friendly and attentive. It was a very busy night, and you could just see them all going around and making sure that customers were all happy and ok. Great Job Team!

Anyhoo, we are already planning our next visit with the family soon. - I think my cousins & titos would really enjoy it here.

Tuesday 19th August, 2025
Between 6pm - 8pm

First time trying this and I'm surprised that this is the best filo restaurant I've ever been to.
I normally don't eat Filipino food or cuisine but this place is quite delicious.
In comparison to the filo restaurant in Lidcombe near the train station on a one way road, this place is heaps better.

Service by staffs were excellent
Food had aroma and flavour
Things like Sisig, kare kare, halo halo, crispy pata
Their steam rice had this aroma coming out of it (most steam rice are bland, but this one you could literally smell the aroma from the rice. It's not like other steam rice's you have out there somewhere else).
Quite a cosy atmosphere and comfortable dining, but I think in my opinion the tables were too high for the seating on the sofa/lounge seats.
(Table 19).
Seat were kind of too low, making the table seem like it's too high.

Only biggest downfall  was that the dishes were quite exy.
Had paid $127 total
Can't believe the halo halo cup....A CUP... Was $15!!!!.
I guess maybe it's because it's the price of the ingredients in the cup.
But it was good.

Came here for lunch, food was really nice expensive when it comes to the other Filipino food I had InThe past few days.

The fish was amazing! Dipping sauces were great.

I loved the fish belly soup sour but amazing!

My friend ordered some kind of pork intestine, fried they were actually really yummy.

The pig’s intestines and crispy pata at Kowboy Grill were very yummy! The intestines were well-cleaned, flavorful, and perfectly cooked, while the crispy pata was crunchy on the outside and tender and juicy on the inside. The live band was everything I ever wanted especially if the restaurant serves delicious food.
The food is delicious, the crew are very friendly, and its great for Friday night out with friends. The singers are excellent and really add to the fun atmosphere.

Lazza Marrickville
address: 246 Marrickville Rd, Marrickville NSW 2204, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Over the past couple of days, my boyfriend and I had been craving good Filipino food, in particular, adobo. Being in North Sydney, quality Filipino food can be hard to come by, so we decided to head out to Marrickville to try Lazza, a place we’ve had our eye on for a while after hearing great things from friends who sing there.

From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with smiles, great vibes, and the smell of authentic Filipino food. They mentioned that adobo isn’t normally on the menu, and unfortunately they had just run out by the time we arrived but that wasn’t an issue at all. They went out of their way to cook an incredible pork adobo just for us, and wow… it genuinely brought us straight back to the Philippines.

On top of that, we had the crispy lechon kawali and the sisig. Both dishes were packed with flavour and tasted incredibly authentic.
If you’re looking for good vibes, great company, amazing food, and live singing, we highly recommend a visit to Lazza Marrickville.

My partner and I never had a bad experience here. Consistently delicious food and fantastic service. They have the best crispy sisig in Sydney! I also loved their bangus, it was cooked to perfection. Can’t wait to be back.
24 dollars，pork sisig ，take away just one tiny small box like children pack.no forks ，mad about where is the egg. It does not match with dine-in photos👀

A lovely filipino restaurant in Marrickville. Been here a few times and I have found the food to be quite good. Had the pork sisig which was good as I thought they may put more fat than pork meat but glad it was nearly all pork meat. Pork BBQ and the Lechon kuwali were tasty. The Kare Kare was presented well but was quite bland as we had to ask for more shrimp paste to add flavour. The halo halo dessert was generous in size and can feed two. My favourite was their Turon (Fried banana) with the ube ice cream. Perfectly crispy without skimping on the banana. The staff are always welcoming and friendly.

Some improvements :-
We were disappointed one evening, on a Friday night with a 6pm sitting, and they ran out of turon for dessert. Very disappointed that they did not cater for a busy evening.
I also do hope their toilet facilities have been fixed as we were surprised that the toilet plumbing wasnt completely functioning.
Please do not advertise that there is live entertainment on Fridays when there has never been any entertainment, the 3 times we have eaten there on a Friday evening. The live entertainment was a reason we chose Friday instead of a different evening.

Overall, a lovely, quaint and charming filipino restaurant.

We got the sizzling chicken plate, (chicken sisig) the deep fried pork belly and a rice. Flavour for chicken was good, but got a bit dry. The pork belly was a little oily. Overall, flavour was ok but price was fairly steep so value was just alright. Might come back occasionally.

reviews:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Leoncia’s – Castle Towers

Hands down the best Filipino food in Castle Towers! The dishes are always fresh, flavourful, and taste just like home. The servings are generous, and the atmosphere is warm and welcoming.

Their desserts and drinks are seriously addictive — you’ll keep coming back for more! We absolutely love the staff here too; they’re always friendly, helpful, and make the whole experience even better.

Leoncia’s is consistent, delicious, and authentically Filipino. Highly recommended! 💯🔥

We ordered from Leoncia’s and honestly, everything was amazing. The Dinuguan was so rich and flavorful and the Pork BBQ was super tender, and packed with taste. For dessert, we tried the Ube Halo-Halo and the U-Bae Gelato Tub, and wow... both were absolutely delicious! Don’t forget the calamansi juice which was the perfect balance of sweet and sour, so so refreshing. You can really tell everything was made with care and the staff were absolutely lovely and accommodating. Highly recommended!

First, the caldereta meal is delicious. I mean really super delicious. The only Filipino resto that’s generous with their servings with friendly ambiance. The gelato is Messina-grade or even better. It’s like Magnolia’s Gold Label but only better. The cassava cake goes well with the gelato. Definitely not a one-hit wonder like you have visited in the west. No Filipino restaurant in Australia offers a $2 calamansi juice.

Surely am coming back next time for their Bihon!

It is not just a filipino dessert place, you can also try filipino hot dishes.

We tried their palabok and sisig, unfortunately both dishes were so good and delicious we forgot to take a photo of the dishes. I would recommend trying both dishes.

We also bought the calamansi juice. You wont be disappointed with the perfect balance of sweetness and sourness of the drink.

The owner and the staff are so accommodating and with a funny and bubbly personality.

It was a worthwhile trip going here.

Experience a great hospitality from the owner, Leoncia, here at Castle Towers.  Authentic and homely taste from every dish - caldereta, palabok and calamari juice.
We had sampler tastings of home made ice cream flavours, jams, and spreads which was nice.
Worth coming back for more dishes.

description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
address: 6, Shopping Centre, Shop 454, Level 3/14 Castle St, Castle Hill NSW 2154, Australia
name: Leoncia's Filipino Desserts & Yema
Let’s Chon
address: 7 Gleeson Ave, Sydenham NSW 2044, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I brought my non Filipino friends to try Filipino food, specifically to try Lechon and they loved it!

Highly recommend their Cebu lechon pork belly. Seasoned well, ,cooked even better with a great crunch and colour on the skin. My friends absolutely loved it! Especially with the vinegar and all purpose sauce.

Recommend their garlic rice and lechon Sisig. The rice was made very well and really hit all the garlic flavour notes and textures. The Lehon Sisig was great as it didn't have too much mayo and really let the lechon flavour shine. There is a surcharge on the egg but it's a must have. The chillis also gave it a nice kick.

Don't recommend the chicken Inasal and palabok. While both were tasty, the chicken Inasal was dry, whereas the palabok didn't have the sauciness of most other palaboks and was definitely lacking in meat and toppings.

Service was warm and prompt with the food coming out at an acceptable wait time. Price wise, it's a little on the expensive side for Filipino restaurants, however, we will definitely be back for more lechon and tol try some of their other offerings!

For people who love Filipino Roast Pig called lechon, Let's Chon is the place to go.  They serve Cebu Lechon known as the savory roast pig of Visayas. Also serving other Filipino bbq and dishes. A quaint place near Sydenham Train Station.

Amazing flavours all the way! There was a third party app promo so decided to give them a try. The crackling skin on the lechon was delightful, however we were given a full plate of lean meat which was a bit of a let down. Would have appreciated a mix between lean and some fatty parts. Garlic fried rice and pinakbet vege dish complimented the lechon nicely and we had an enjoyable meal overall.

If you enjoy decent Philippines food then it's a good option.
I love the smell of the chargrilled rotisserie and the aroma of spice's.
The wait staff are pleasant, as are most Philipino folks.
Definitely worth a try
The milk fish I had was excellent but the portions were quite small in general. I also appreciated the variety of condiments in the sauce station and the staff's recommendation on sauce pairings.

address: 7 Gleeson Ave, Sydenham NSW 2044, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
name: Let’s Chon
Little Angel Cafe
address: 267 Victoria Rd, Rydalmere NSW 2116, Australia
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
reviews:
The best oat matcha latte in town!

Seriously, so delicious, my previous experiences with matcha cafes had me feeling like I've drank grass, but this one was so gooood. It wasnt overly sweet, had that actual matcha taste and was economical.

The onigiri wasnt as good, it crumbled the moment I took it out of the packaging, and the rice tasted old (hard), but the filling was yummy.

The cafe itself is small, with limited seats, and it shares a space with a guitar store (kinda cool).

Would def come back here again!
The cafe is small and cute. I tried the iced matcha ube drink and their onigiris (tuna mayo and yuzu salmon). Both were tasty and with generous fillings. The staff were also friendly and welcoming 😊.

I visit here every chance I get… which isn’t often, but each time I do, it doesn’t disappoint. Ya’ll already know the food and drink slaps, (my personal favs: ham cheese croissy, salmon onigiri, banana matcha) but lemme talk about something else.. I love all the effort that’s been put in to make it an inclusive space, from experimental, diverse ingredients (e.g honey dill / adobo) to the way customers are treated (e.g Polaroid fridge, always keen to laugh) Everything feels so intentional, even down to the origin of the cafe’s name – the meaning behind it is so thoughtful 💙 p.s never get rid of the onigiri pleaaaaase 🙏

If I could give more stars, I would!

The coffee was bold, smooth and really well executed. The ube matcha left me speechless and the Chicken Katsu Sando was filling and amazing balance with the flavours.

Not only am I super pleased with the food and drink, the most important thing happened - I was served with a smile and very efficiently!

It makes me sad that I don’t live in the area and it’s not my local but I know where my pit stop will be every time I’m out this way!

This little cafe tucked away in the north of Parra is for sure a hidden gem! The Ube latte and Banana Matcha was the best I’ve had so far in Sydney! Definitely a must try and also their katsu Sando was a good snack to go along with the drinks!

Can’t wait to come back to try their filo x Japanese fusion food in the future!

name: Little Angel Cafe
description: A verified Filipino cafe in Sydney.
address: 267 Victoria Rd, Rydalmere NSW 2116, Australia

name: Lola Remy's
address: Shop 1/56 Lakeside Parade, Jordan Springs NSW 2747, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: This place is a local favourite of ours, we visit almost every other week for takeaway- they also have some dine in seats available as well as a small selection of Asian grocery items. We love the food here, we regularly order the lechon, adobo, bbq skewers and caldereta and everything is always great. The staff here are beyond kind and always treat me and my family with kindness. Everything is truly good and I will always regularly visit.

Great Filipino food, definitely some of the best around! The flavors are amazing, though I do wish the trading hours were longer on weekends. Also, I’d love it if the pork adobo was available on the menu all the time- it’s that good!

Lovely Pilipino stop for food and shopping. The place is clean and organised. We had lunch food is so yummy, specially the sisig. Price is good 2 choice and rice $15. We did some Pilipino goodies shopping too. The owner is there. He is very nice and welcoming. Resonate of shops back home. And big THANK YOU for displaying our flyer and helping out small business. Cannot wait to be back and tell friends too!
I was pretty nervous cos I don't eat alot of fillipino  food. I had pork merchado very yummy massive portion the spring rolls fantastic . Defs going back . Thanks for making it easy
Came here to eat and lovely owners. Cozy vibes and authentic filipino food. Would recommend 10 outta 10, will be back again!

description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
name: Lola Remy's
address: Shop 1/56 Lakeside Parade, Jordan Springs NSW 2747, Australia

name: Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery
address: 15 Cleeve Cl, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Friendly and warm service. We found Lola Rizza's food to be well flavoured; unlike many other Filipino places, the dishes weren't overly salty. Prices are reasonable for the generous portion sizes. We ordered from the hot bar and the food was expectedly not freshly prepared nor served hot. They were genuine in their desire to help us choose and generously explained the dishes and offered to make us try them.
We will need to return to fully experience them all!

Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery is a amazing quality food restaurant and a great place to enjoy a delightful meal. The Service is outstanding and the food is a unique cuisine. Either at home or at the restaurant it still leaves you with a full stomach.
This restaurant has excellent expectations when you walk in, this is also a family owned business.
(My friend is the grandson of the owner "Noah")

Thank you

We ordered dinuguan, barbecue, and chicken inasal via uber eats. They were all lacking flavour. The dinuguan had good texture but lacked sourness. The barbecue lacked sweetness and was a bit thick. The chicken inasal lacked sweetness too. The only one that tasted nice was the free soup they gave.

Mum, auntie and I had lunch here, having the two choice and rice. The pork sigsig diced pork with calamansi, chilli and red onions. The kare kare had no peanut taste. The bbq pork were $5 each, but they were big portions. They served us complementary chicken soup.

The reason why I put it so low rating is because my mum found a big piece of plastic inside her Springroll but thankfully they made her meal free of charge. 🤷🏽‍♂️ $58+ in total

My 2nd time eating here. Inasal is to die for!!!! Love the marinate and the sauce- chili toyo mansi. Love the lechon kawali as well served with mang thomas. Everything tasted nice and would definitely recommend to my friends 🩷

description: A verified Filipino shop in Sydney.
name: Lola Rizza's Filipino Eatery
address: 15 Cleeve Cl, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia

name: Lola's Table Filipino Mexican Bistro Wentworth Point
address: Shop 1/18 Footbridge Bvd, Wentworth Point NSW 2127, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
It’s been awhile since I had a dish that surprise me. But the elote / corn was 🤯🤯

Corn: 6/5
Pig trotters: 5/5
Caramelised pork: 5/5
Halo: 5/5

The corn was really mind blowing. I’ve not tasted corn like this before and was just wowwed. It was so juicy and tasty and charred! Perfectly done.

The size of the pig trotter dish just got us laughing when it came, cause it was so big 😂 we brought home half of it…

The caramelised pork dish has a tangy flavour to it which was really nice too.

My husband rate the place 4/5 just cos we’re not very used to the flavours, and tbh, I don’t usually consume so much meat but I can’t think of how they can make the dishes any better hence, I give them 5/5 for all the dishes. One improvement is if they can have some vegetables for the caramelised pork dish cause the whole meal was super meaty without much veg.

The small halo portion was also pretty big!

We paid $78 in total, and brought home a lot of it. Seems like we’re not the only one cos most of the tables around us were also getting boxes for takeaway 😂

Only thing is, staff serving can ideally know their fridge desserts better as they didn’t really know how to explain the ingredients in there or what they were (we are not Filipinos so some food introduction would be helpful).

Also, a large group came in and sat behind us so the restaurant got really noisy after and not conducive for conversations. Perhaps some sound proofing would be helpful.

Nonetheless, I was very impressed by the meal - esp for a cuisine we don’t usually frequent. Thank you for the yummy food and mind blowing corn!! 😍

I'm always keen to try Filipino food as I have not always had a great experience. Lola's Table was also unique in that it is marketed as a Filipino / mexican fusion restaurant.

We did order from the filipino side of the menu - we'll be back to try the mexican fusion.

We had the Crispy pata - served with soft tacos :-) and guacamole as well as the usual vinegar soy sauce dip.
Spring rolls and the Daing na Bangus (boneless milkfish)

They had excellent service with staff friendly and approachable. In terms of the food the crispy pata was delish - just what I expected and it was crispy!  The spring rolls reminded me of my mums spring rolls. The boneless milkfish could have been done better. it was very well seasoned on one side but not the other and a tad overcooked.  The seasoning would have been great if it was evenly on the fish but it made half the fish super sour and salty and the other half not.

They had a lot more things on the menu we couldn't try as there was only 2 of us. Great for groups for family style sharing. They also did catering which was good to know for future events at home.

Amazing food!
Their crispy pata was definitely very crispy on the outside and the meat was tender in the inside
The fish tacos had a good kick to them
The chicken nachos were so yum as well!

Atmosphere was okay, I believe they open about 5pm. We got there at 6, outdoor setting were not yet set up. No one outside to greet to we just took our seats.
However, staff were lovely. Very kind.
My partner loves this place...gets the pork knuckle EVERY TIME...
Also had another traditional Philippino dish and was delish.
We even tried a couple desserts. Very different but sooo good.
BYO also.

This was our first time to eat in Lolas Table.
Reservation was done ahead of time. They were very accommodating when we adjusted the time due to unexpected activities to attend.

The food was great, we were so full!
We choose boodle fight B and ordered nilagang baka for our soup since there are 3 kids.
The crispy pata was so crunchy and yummy.
The kaldereta was good too.
My Mom only complained on the sisig because it was kinda spicy 🥵 chilly. Hahaha.

They assist us well and attended on our needs.
I encourage everyone to visit their restaurant.

description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
address: Shop 1/18 Footbridge Bvd, Wentworth Point NSW 2127, Australia
name: Lola's Table Filipino Mexican Bistro Wentworth Point
Lugaw Queen Restaurant
address: 207 Liverpool Rd, Ashfield NSW 2131, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Of all the Filipino food I’ve been able to find outside of western Sydney this place is the most accurate to the flavours I grew up with. Pales in comparison to what you’d get further out west but the food actually tasted like home without trying to pretentiously re-invent Filipino food to be on trend.
Had the loaded beef pares which came with lechon kawali, chicharon bulaklak and a side of soup (the nice server asked if I wanted the soup later so I said yes but they should just go ahead and give the soup regardless because it’s my favourite part of the meal). The flavours of the beef pares were more subtle than other ones I’ve had but the soup was deeply beefy and flavourful so balanced the meal.

Place was decorated exactly how my mum would do a place up if she had free rein over interior design. The smell of scented candles they had on the tables was a little dizzying but the ambience was nice. Maybe non scented candles next time?

Other reviews complain of service and whilst it wasn’t rude, I did have to call out to pay the bill but that’s probably another bonus for the place’s authenticity (kids running around the back included)

So-so experience. Ordered the chicken inasal and it tasted more like java chicken timpla rather than legit inasal. Disappointed though that the initial meal and drink I wanted wasn’t available. Service and friendliness can be improved as well.

wonderful ambience and great authentic filipino food, our big fambam enjoyed all theie food; unlike soup, kare kare and hipon siniganggang was such a big hit! Husband and i also ordered their Lomi overload and mami overload perfect for them winter chilli nights! Only request please add sweet and sour spicey sauce for fish balls and kikiam, soy/ vinegar for crispy pata & more Ice tea please!
Can’t wait to go back and tey your halo halo
Lovely place. Make sure you come in early because they run out of food pretty quickly especially Lugaw! Lomi is superb and given in generous servings too.
Food tastes great. Home Filipino food. Not bad at all.

MRT Pilipino Foods and Products
address: Shop C10, 5/9 Freedom plaza, Cabramatta NSW 2166, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
This is a first for Cabramatta, a Filipino store walked past it and noticed it has all the products that a Filipino person would need.

They also have a hot bar outside servicing hot delicacies as well from their motherland.

Can't complain the more variety of choices the better.
Heaps of yummy dishes available at affordable price. Most of Philippine products are there. Very friendly staff and good customer service. 100 % satisfied!
I really like shopping here as last time I was looking for Reno liver paste and I can't find it anywhere except here in MRT. They have cooked foods that you can eat as well.
Glad to have U MRT pinoy shop at cabramatta...Only one n first Pinoy shop @ cabramatta. Affordable prices n most of all humble and friendly owners ❤️❤️ MUST try to shop in mga kababayan!!!

wonderful/beautiful owners, unbeatable service , great/yummy/fulfilling Filipino dish!
a must visit store for ALL!
especially homesick(ed) pinoys

name: Maison Mornings
address: 2/72 Tennyson Rd, Mortlake NSW 2137, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
A bright, warm and welcoming cafe that serve amazing food ! Was definitely surprised and excited about the Filipino dishes on the menu and had to try it. The pork adobo was so incredibly tender and delicious that I could have more of it! It was cooked amazingly well and the vinegar to soy sauce ratio was perfect. The dish was cooked with hearty intention ! And of course, the honey glazed pork was to die for. Portion and presentation was nailed down, and kudos to the chef because the meat was incredibly tender and delicious.  Coffee was great too, and the customer service was friendly and welcoming ! :) The place is also dog-friendly, and has small outdoor seating.

Overall, a beautiful gem in Mortlake, and would definitely come back :) Maison Mornings was definitely a great start to my morning !

Very nice and relaxing vibe. Beautifully presented dishes with a generous volume. However, the syrup was fully poured in the pancake plate making the lower part too sweet and a bit soggy. The mushroom pasta was also too heavy on the cream and light on taste. The service was quick enough. However, the two ladies in the shop were spending more time in the back or talking together rather than being around.

Great little place. Had lots of good options for food. The coffee we got was excellent. The milk is hot and silky. The pasta we ordered had truffle but not so much truffle. You couldn't taste anything else and was absolutely delicious. The service there was excellent. Staff are friendly to us and our children who were perfectly behaved. The table that we sat at had a nook for the pram which is excellent. A nook!?!  The whole place felt inviting and comfortable - and the staff helped us by taking our order at the table, I'm sure it's meant to be that I go up to the counter, but they saw we were a bit disorganised and helped us out.
Would go again for sure.

Maison Mornings is truly a gem in the neighbourhood. My wife and I go there every morning for coffee, and we always feel so welcomed — Ivy, Jason and the team greet us (and even our dog Poppy!) by name, which is such a lovely personal touch.

The coffee is hands down the best in the area, and the breakfast menu never disappoints — I’m especially a fan of the eggs Benny, it’s a go-to favourite. The café itself is beautifully designed with a calm, inviting atmosphere and always the perfect background music to start the day right.

All the staff are friendly, thoughtful, and seem to genuinely enjoy getting to know their regulars. Maison Mornings has become a part of our daily routine, and we feel lucky to have it as our local.

Highly recommend!

Went here for a spontaneous brunch date and was super happy with the dishes we ordered. Reasonably priced, super tasty and the owners were lovely.

Definitely recommend visiting. Will be back!!!

name: Maison Mornings
