name: Mama Lor Restaurant & Bakery
address: Shop 5/39-45 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Fresh Lumpia… the filling is just ORANGE and PURPLE SWEET POTATOES. I was expecting vegetables inside… where are the beans carrots sprout beans?
Crispy Palabok… it was dry hardly any sauce in it and have to pay for the extra sauce.
Tapsilog… garlic rice had a plastic taste
We used to enjoy food in this restaurant but now its food standard has gone down to ZERO. What happened ?
We will NEVER come back to this place again.

If you're craving for fried pork belly.. this is the place! It's crunchy and yet melts in your mouth. The adobo is tender and melting.
Their bakery range is a must try too! Ube cheese pandesal!!!

Came for lunch,  I ordered the chicken adobo it was nice, I also ordered the pork soup, this was quite strong and sound once I adapted it was amazing!

My friend ordered the fish and beef, the fish was yummy needs a nicer dip though.

Ordered Beef Pares.  It was disgusting.  Thin fatty bits of what they called beef- it could have been anything.  It did not resemble the photo at all and it was mainly sauce with a few bits of meat in it.  Unfortunately I didn't take a photo.  This was $25 plus another $4 for rice.  And my son feels sick now after the meal. Will never go there again.

I ordered food yesterday for work through Uber Eats and scheduled it for afternoon delivery. As a long-time customer of Mama Lor, I’ve always enjoyed their food and had high expectations.

Unfortunately, this recent experience was a huge letdown—and enough to lose me as a loyal customer.

I ordered the fried chicken because it looked appetizing in the Uber Eats menu photo. But when it arrived, I was shocked. The chicken looked overcooked and very dark. The skin was hard, the taste was bitter, and it had a greasy aftertaste that made it inedible. A friend of mine who is a chef said it likely tasted that way because the oil had been reused too many times. That was clear.

The pancit was also disappointing—extremely oily, to the point where it looked like it could spoil quickly. There was no balance in flavor or presentation. It felt rushed and careless.
To make things worse, I brought the food to share at work, and everyone commented on how unappetizing it was. It was embarrassing. And for the price I paid, it felt like a complete waste.

I spent $46 for the pancit with an additional $4 for the chicken add-on, and $48 for a 10-piece fried chicken. Nearly $100 in total—and not a single dish was worth it. Expensive, but with no substance or quality to justify the cost.

Just because I ordered via Uber Eats doesn’t mean the food should be treated with less care. The standard should be consistent with what’s served in-store, especially for a restaurant I’ve supported for years. This experience didn’t reflect that at all.

I won’t be ordering from Mama Lor again, and I’ll be warning others as well. Very disappointing.

address: Shop 5/39-45 Rooty Hill Rd N, Rooty Hill NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
name: Mama Lor Restaurant & Bakery
Manila Food
address: 20 Main St, Blacktown NSW 2148, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I went here recently at lunch hour as I was hungry looking for an affordable meal.

When I enquired as to what food choices I could have from the food in the food warmer, a woman stated that I could get 2 servings of Filipino food and rice for $16. When I clarified if I heard her correctly, she replied that 2 servings of food and rice in a typical takeaway container was $16. I was surprised.

The shop is not a restaurant and those prices I'd expect at a cafe or restaurant. I ordered pork buns instead but the pastry was slightly stale, the pork was okay.

Would I come here again? If prices were more reasonable, especially in an area such as Blacktown then maybe. I wouldn't order the pork buns ever again.

I wanted Sisig, I paid $18.50 for it and it is DISGUSTINGLY FILTHY. It is so hard to chew and I am so disappointed. I want my money back. THIS. IS. NOT. SISIG. Filo Lasa’s Sisig (inside Westpoint Blacktown) is the legitimate one. Do better, Manila Food. I’m embarrassed to call this Filipino food. I will never buy sisig from here ever again. If I can give it 0 stars, I would.

Great staff. Very accomodating, and very good service. Great filipino authentic street food. They also have Ria Money Transfer service.
Take-away and dine-in filipino food stall. 👍
Street parking for half-hour only.
1 min walk from train station.

My husband and I have tried many things from here and I an so impressed! I really enjoyed the spring roll thing with the sweet potato inside. I also got a pork soup and the flavours are amazing! We will keep coming back!

First time trying this Filo store  .
One who were serving us has attitude . Ask a question gave a plain answer . What a great service isn’t it. Seems like she’s not interested at all.  Pls don’t have a business if you have NO proper customer service ettiqute.
Just close your doors .  ON top of that Food has no taste and Overpriced.

reviews: What a great shop and customer service! My mate and I were passing by in the mall and came across this place. We were curious and asked the woman (apologies, I didn't get her name) for some of her favorite recommendations. Will definitely return to try some more new things. Thank you

Super expensive! Walked in expecting a casual browse, walked out feeling like I’d accidentally taken out a small loan. None of the items are tagged with prices, so you’re basically shopping blind until you reach the counter…and that’s where the bill shock hits hard.
By far the most overpriced shop with the rudest staff. Simply terrible customer service. Total disrespect and disregard.

This shop doesnt even deserve a star, not accommodating, rude staff , no price tag! 🙄🙄 PLEASE LEARN HOW TO SAY THANKYOU AND HI HELLO ???????  ITS EVEN PRICEY AND NOT EVEN WORTH TO VISIT UR SHOP!!!!!! ALWAYS USING PHONE WHILE THERE’s a customer needs to pay!!! LOL

This store is unbeliveable not professional, price is expensive, the item is not sometimes available. In addtition, they always open not ON-TIME, says 10.30 am on the website but the staff comes around 11 or whatever they like.

name: Manuel's Kitchen
address: 126 Hampden Rd, Artarmon NSW 2064, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We found this Filipino café through a Google search, and the food was amazing! We ordered the chicken inasal, beef tapa, and pork BBQ skewers—everything was delicious.

Kuya Manuel was incredibly welcoming, which gave the café such a warm, homey vibe. We’ll definitely be coming back!

We stumbled upon this gem by accident while searching Google Maps for Filipino food near Chatswood.

Manuel is such a warm and hospitable host—he even asked us to try the 'puto' before our meal arrived. I recommend saving it for dessert (or taking it home), as the portion is huge!

We ordered the BBQ and Chicken Inasal—both were excellent, and the BBQ was a standout; my kids said it was one of the best they've ever tried.

We finished with 'halo-halo' that didn't skimp on the toppings at all. The portion is generous enough to easily share between two people.

All in all, highly recommended—we'll definitely be back!

Finally got the chance to visit Manuel’s Kitchen yesterday, and it was well worth the wait! (We would’ve come earlier during the holiday break, but they were closed from Dec 24–26.)

Manuel greeted us warmly and took great care of us throughout our visit, which made the experience even more special.

My family and I loved everything we ordered — the longsilog, bangsilog, pork BBQ, and inihaw na baboy were all spot on.

I was tempted to order the pata tim too, especially after hearing friends rave about it, but I didn’t have anyone to share it with. At least that gives me a good excuse to come back!

Loved the relaxed, chill vibe. Perfect for a late Sunday morning — or honestly, any day.

Food was very good and service super friendly. The star of the show for me was the chicken inasal, super tender and juicy and the adobo rice was delicious. Pork bbq skewers were good too. Will come back.

MANUEL’S KITCHEN — Artarmon, Sydney

Stepping into Manuel’s Kitchen feels like walking into the heart of Filipino hospitality — where food is more than a meal; it’s a story of love, comfort, and celebration.

Every dish served here reflects Chef Manuel’s passion, craftsmanship, and deep respect for Filipino flavours. It’s a culinary experience that speaks to both heart and heritage.

Pata Tim — A masterpiece. The pork was fall-off-the-bone tender, luxuriously glazed, and beautifully seasoned. The portion was generous — a true feast to share. Each bite melts effortlessly, reminding you why this classic remains a Filipino favourite.

Kare-Kare — A comforting beef stew immersed in rich, nutty peanut sauce. The flavours were balanced and soulful — perfectly paired with bagoong (shrimp paste) that brings out that savoury depth we all crave.

Chicken Inasal — Juicy, chargrilled to perfection, and marinated with that signature Visayan tang. Dip it in vinegar and you’ll understand what home tastes like — smoky, zesty, and deeply satisfyin

Pork BBQ — Simply perfect. Tender, caramelised, and bursting with flavour — every skewer evokes the joy of Filipino street food, elevated with finesse.

Halo-Halo — A delightful finale. Not overly sweet — just right. A refreshing medley of textures and colours that cools the soul and crowns the meal with happiness.

Every dish at Manuel’s Kitchen tells a story — of tradition, resilience, and pride. Chef Manuel has truly created a space where Filipino food takes its rightful place on Sydney’s culinary map.

For those who long for the taste of home — or anyone ready to discover the heart of Filipino cuisine — Manuel’s Kitchen in Artarmon is a must-visit.

We’re in Australia, and it’s important for us to improve our English since it’s the main language used to communicate with people of different nationalities.

I’m really disappointed with your staff’s attitude. You should know how to treat and communicate with your customers better!

So the owner turned off the comment section because they couldn’t come up with a better reason to respond.

Don’t try to fool me—I know your shop. When Mang Jigs was managing it, there were no issues like this because he knew how to treat customers well.
And what do you mean you don’t have an older lady staff member? She herself claimed to own the relyeno dish you’re selling.

description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: My wife ordered a cake and discovered the establishment was under new ownership. The new owner was Jeremiah, who was very accommodating and very courteous. He had carried his people skills from his previous profession in the hotel industry to this business venture that he has engaged in. The cake we normally order is a traditional mocha cake that reminds us of the good old days of Goldilocks Bakery in the Philippines. We’ve been a patron of Melchrishel’s Bakery from the time of its very first owners and the flavour of our favorite cake has not changed. Keep up the great standard Jeremiah. Your brand is truly an icon to the Filipino community here at OZ.

Breakfast and meryenda sorted! We’ve visited Melchrishel Bakery three times in the past few weeks because my wife and I genuinely enjoy their baked goods.

Our favourites so far are the pandesal, Spanish bread, ensaymada with cheese, and mamon. It's always fresh, soft, and full of flavour. We’re looking forward to trying more of their selection next time.

I also learned that the bakery is now under new management, and you can really see the improvements each time we visit. It’s great to see the effort being put into making the place better.

If you may have felt disappointed during a visit a month or so ago, I’d suggest giving them another try. Based on our recent experiences, there’s been noticeable improvement and we’ve been very happy each time.

Big thanks to Jeremiah and the team for bringing the authentic taste of a Filipino bakery to Sydney!

If you’re craving something that tastes like home, this Filipino bakery in Sydney is a must-visit 🇵🇭✨
The breads and pastries instantly gave me a wave of nostalgia — every bite brought me straight back to childhood merienda afternoons. Soft, freshly baked, and full of that authentic flavor you can’t fake.
The new owners are incredibly warm and welcoming, and you can really feel the care they put into everything they make. They’re also working on expanding their menu with more traditional and creative recipes, which makes me even more excited to keep coming back.
Overall, such a gem. If you love Filipino baked goods (or want to try them for the first time), this spot is 100% worth checking out. 💛🍞🥐

Panlasang Pinoy
address: 26 Regent St, Kogarah NSW 2217, Australia
description: Straightforward restaurant spotlighting roasted fish dishes, plus classic rice meals.
reviews:
From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with warm smiles and familiar aromas that instantly transported us back to the Philippines. The welcoming atmosphere and friendly service definitely set a positive tone.

There was a good variety on offer, and many of the dishes were enjoyable, especially the classic Filipino comfort foods. However, even as Filipinos, we felt some dishes were lacking a bit of depth and balance. A few items could have benefited from stronger seasoning, more freshness, and better consistency, as some dishes leaned slightly dry or underwhelming compared to traditional home-style flavours. The buffet setup also meant some dishes had been sitting for a while, which affected temperature and texture.

Overall, it’s a decent introduction to Filipino cuisine and a convenient option if you’re craving familiar flavours, but it didn’t quite hit the mark in terms of authenticity and wow factor for us. Still worth trying, especially for those new to Filipino food, but with room for improvement.

A must visit for your Filo cravings. Also best place to replenish your Filo pantry. Available almost everything. Tasty food and friendly staff. I love their dinuguan and barbecue, very Pinoy taste! A little pricey on the Filo goods. I hope they can just put price tags to easy accounting. Street car parking available.
There is no soap, leftover that cost $30, not worth the hype and $35 each per person, no veggie dish, and also limited seating. Overall, not recommended! Go to another Filipino Buffet!
food was fine but service and experience misleading. went to eat on a thursday, but was charged weekend rates & our whole group was overcharged $30 for no reason.

Wonderful family style Pinoy buffet serving home cooked style that your grandma used to make. The people who run it are lovely also and very friendly. $30 on weekdays running from lunch time all the way to dinner. And $35 on weekends where Lechon (suckling pig) is added. Will definitely be back here again.

Nice little nook in Kings Park industrial area. The crispy pata dish I ordered was tasty and a good size for my quick lunch. The service was swift and warm. I won’t hesitate coming back here :)

description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: I was in the area and have heard good reviews from friends. The food did not disappoint.  I went for lunch but came back to takeaway some pork skewers, crispy pata and dinuguan which were muy delicioso. Happy tummy!

Ordered a sinigang, lumpia and pancit for my birthday lunch with my partner. The food was absolutely spot on from how my family makes it, every single meal was delicious from what we ordered. The atmosphere is also great. It was a hot weather today which is around 35 but the place counters the heat with its own air-conditioning and music. Really nice place, thank you guys !!!

First time dining here, and the food is fantastic. Made the mistake of ordering 2 x crispy pata. Only 1 is required between 2 people. Went on a Sunday afternoon and there were already customers eating there. Great Filipino food at decent prices.

These lovely people have catered for my Daughters 1st birthday & my sons 15th birthday. On both occasions the staff & the food have been absolutely amazing !!  The staff have been very understanding as my mum who is currently overseas pays for food and I cant thank the staff in being very understanding while serving me while they are busy. Thank you so much !

It was my first time to dine in at Pinoy Grill N Chill, atmosphere is amazing, soft music, I feel at home, service is very good, I feel like I’m eating at home with my family, highly recommend the Crispy Pata, reminded me on how my mother used to cook the Crispy Pata, I love the desserts too, I even had some take aways haha.. I met Ate Cynthia for the first time and I must admit she made me feel like family. The background music brings back some good memories of my childhood. I will certainly go back again. Oh I forgot, they have the best boiled rice too… ube cake is one of the best I ever had too. I could go on and on but I’m afraid that I might ran out of space. I just wanted to take this opportunity as well to say thank you for the lovely and friendly service as well. Job well done!!! I’m proud of you Ate Cynthia!!!!! Keep up the good work. I’m a proud Filipina and it’s so uplifting to see my own kind doing a good job. All the best Ate Cynthia and her team. Compliments to the chef.

reviews:
Very good family restaurant! We ended up sharing a platter I wanted to teach my very western daughter how to eat like Filipino and so we washed our hands and ate with our hands. We also had leftovers, (because of course). The food was good, but honestly? The atmosphere is what sold it.

The karaoke setup was solid and the vibe was warm, loud, and exactly what you want on a rainy night. Romi was singing when we were there and she absolutely understood the assignment 😍her voice was on point, energy up, and the whole place felt better because of her. Even if you’re not singing, it’s great just to sit back and enjoy.

Service was friendly and relaxed, not rushed, not awkward. By the end we were overstuffed, happy, and very aware that unbuttoning pants was the correct choice.

If you want Filipino food with proper karaoke energy and a fun, welcoming vibe, this place delivers. Would 100% go back, especially if Romi’s singing again.

Excellent food and awesome service..
Trisha is nice person and very accomodating.
Our first time to dine in here in Pinoy Lounge.  We're coming back to try other food.

The Filipino food was absolutely delicious — the crispy rolls were golden and crunchy on the outside with a soft, flavorful filling, the grilled stuffed squid was smoky, juicy, and perfectly seasoned with onions and peppers, and the stir-fried vermicelli noodles were light, tasty, and full of fresh vegetables and seafood. Every dish had an authentic taste and generous portion, making the whole meal so satisfying and worth coming back for! ✨ will come again

Came here by Googling "filipino restaurant near me", and I wish it was actually nearer to me cause the food is amazing.
Staff were attentive and gave excellent recommendations. Variety of food selection with generous servings, all very fresh. Masarap 🫶

My photos do not do the food justice.
The Sisig was crispy, zesty,  rich, with a slight kick, and  morish.

reviews:
I’ve ordered from Pinoy station a few times and their food are true to the Fillo taste! I’ve tried both catering size & take away serving and they are generous with the quantity provided. My husband and in-laws (who aren’t Filipinos) like their dish too, especially the Beef Caldereta, Chicken Spring rolls, Pancit Palabok, Turon (Banana spring rolls), Tortang talong (Fried eggplant with egg), Embutido, Fried Tilapia, BBQ Pork and Leche Flan. I highly recommend them.

What I also appreciate is that when I requested for BBQ Pork, which was not on their menu, Jerome provided it for me without any hesitation. That is a mark of a true excellent customer service! I will defo order again! Thank you Jerome, Marissa and Team for always giving us an A+ service and products! :)

Smelt really good, decided to dine in.
$15 for 2 dishes, $20 for 3 dishes.
Portion was small, they were served on palm sized plates.
We had peanut sauce beef, pork belly and pork with white rice.
Pork was too salty to finish.
Place was also pretty filthy with food scraps, cockroaches on the floor.
There was extended seating area behind the store itself to accommodate more customers but it seemed like they hardly require that space.
2 stars for the fragrance and home cooked family business, perhaps the other dishes were great but would not dine here again.

Their Pansit Malabon was absolutely delicious! I will definitely order again in the future and also try other dishes. My order was ready on time, and the staff gave excellent customer service. Maraming salamat po.
Whenever I come here I get the giant spring roll (can’t remember the name!) and the pansit. Never disappoints. Kind servers and reasonable prices.

name: Sir Manong Eastern Creek
address: Shop 3501/159 Rooty Hill Rd S, Eastern Creek NSW 2766, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We ordered crispy pata and the torta,  have to say both dishes were amazing!!! The pata was crispy enough but the meat wasn't dry but moist.

The torta you need to experience, full of flavour, the dried kale on top added a nice dynamic which changed the flavour profile Im used to with my wife's version.

Highly recommend!!!!

Sir Manong’s is always a must-visit for us whenever we’re in Sydney. We genuinely go out of our way to eat here because the food has never failed to impress. We’ve been in Australia for over two years now, and I’ve already celebrated two of my birthdays here — and both times, this restaurant has been part of those special memories. That alone says a lot about how much we love this place.

Our usual orders include classics like kare kare and binagoongan, and they are consistently outstanding. The meat is always tender, perfectly cooked, and full of flavour, while the sauces are rich, balanced, and comforting — exactly how Filipino food should be. Every dish feels thoughtfully prepared, and you can really taste the care that goes into the cooking.

Another thing we really appreciate is how family-friendly this branch is. While we wait for our food, our son always enjoys playing with other kids in the small play area just outside the restaurant. It makes the wait feel shorter and more relaxed for everyone, especially for families dining with young children.

The service is just as good as the food. Staff are always friendly, welcoming, and happy to help with recommendations, which makes the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

Overall, Sir Manong’s holds a special place for us. It’s not just about the food — it’s about the memories we’ve made there. If you’re looking for consistently great Filipino food in Sydney, this is the branch we’d recommend every time.

If you’re missing Filipino food, this place is a must-visit. The food is comforting, flavourful, and really hits that home-style Filipino taste. I also loved the ambiance—warm, relaxed, and welcoming.

The only downside is the service, which could be improved, as the staff sometimes forget to accommodate requests for utensils and plates.

Overall, still a great spot for Filipino food, especially when you’re craving something familiar and hearty. 🇵🇭🍽️

Sir Manong Filipino Cuisine brings a true taste of the Philippines to the table, with food that is undeniably tasty and full of flavor. The dishes capture the richness and heartiness of Filipino cooking—savory meats, comforting soups, and well-seasoned classics that make the restaurant a worthwhile stop for anyone craving authentic flavors.

🍴 Food

The highlight here is definitely the food. Each dish is prepared with care, offering that satisfying balance of sweet, sour, and savory that defines Filipino cuisine. From grilled specialties to stews and rice plates, the menu is generous, delicious, and sure to leave you feeling full and happy.

🪑 Seating & Atmosphere

That said, the seating isn’t the most comfortable, and the overall atmosphere can feel a bit cramped. Add to that the fact that the space often gets noisy, and it may not be the best place for those looking for a calm or cozy dining experience.

👩‍🍳 Service

The staff are friendly, greeting you warmly and creating a welcoming vibe. However, they are not always very helpful, and service can sometimes feel inattentive or rushed. It’s not unfriendly, but it doesn’t add much to the dining experience either.

⸻

✅ In short: Sir Manong Filipino Cuisine delivers where it matters most—the food is tasty and authentic—but the comfort, noise level, and service could be improved. It’s a place best enjoyed if you’re going mainly for the flavors rather than the setting.

A wise Filipino once told me that if you feel a little bit dizzy after a massive Filipino meal, you'll know that food hit good! Something about high blood pressure from too much crispy pork 😅. That was my colleague from work who told me that, and I put her theory out to the test!
Many Filipinos have told me they love the food at Sir Manong in Eastern Creek. If there's two must order dishes here for me, they'd be the crispy pata and the BBQ chicken. Crispy pata is a twice-cooked pork knuckle, which is first stewed in seasoning and spices before being deep-fried to make the skin as crispy as possible. A mountain of crackling gets served to your table, enclosing layers of soft, flavourful meat underneath. The BBQ chicken is marinated and brined before being cooked over charcoal on a rotating spit. The meat turns out incredibly juicy, but I'd say the best part of the chicken is the tangy, savoury crispy skin. Both dishes get served with pickled red cabbage and a soy vinegar dipping sauce. These were just a joy to eat, and the sweet-and-sour fireworks in your mouth from both are definitely worth the temporary pressure rise 😂
Some dishes are done traditionally here, while others have a modernised touch. Take the sisig for example - this was my first time seeing sisig with a soft-cooked egg and extra crackling on top. I probably do prefer it on the usual big oval sizzling plate though. The bottom layer would get nice and crispy as the meat cooks further in its own fat. I loved the pork binagoongan fried rice! This was my first time trying it, and wow, the flavours on this one were intense. It's fried in the wok with fermented krill and, I think shrimp paste. The fried rice has smokey shellfish flavour throughout, almost like there was crab and prawn meat in it. It's served with eggplant and crackling skin twice-cooked pork belly on top. Another +1 on the pressure train, but again so very delicious it's forgiveable.
P.S. The calamansi juice here is a must. It's uber refreshing after all of that fried pork, you'll need it!
📍 Sir Manong, Eastern Creek

We also bought some sweets for take away, we loved the ube cake, letche flan and chicken macaroni salad. Staffs were very friendly and accomodating. Highly recommend to try their food especially the sweets🤩

The filo flavours here are incredible—so rich and satisfying. The prices are on the higher side but it was worth it, the staff are lovely. Be prepared to wait as the place can get busy. Highly recommend !!

The food is outstanding, bursting with flavor and expertly prepared. Each dish showcases a great balance of spices and ingredients, making every bite a satisfying experience.

Staffs are friendly too!

The ambiance is warm and inviting. Overall, it is a fantastic choice for anyone looking to satisfy their filo food cravings with delicious food and great service. Highly recommended!

reviews: Such a cute space in Doonside! Came here for drinks and ended up getting some bites to eat! The guy who served us was lovely and we thank him for his hospitality. Definitely recommended coming here for the sweet and savory options!

The best Basque Cheesecake is sold here!!! ) AAA+++
The texture is just right & the consistency of the cheese is sublime!

5-Star Review – Starlight Bakery
Visited: 12 April 2025

We visited Starlight Bakery with a group of 18, and everyone had a great time! The staff were warm, welcoming, and served us with genuine smiles, which made the experience even better.

This Filipino bakery has been part of the Western Sydney community for over 25 years and is clearly loved by both the Filipino community and many others.

The food and drinks were fresh and delicious. The ube buns were a standout — soft, tasty, and served quickly. Everyone in our group enjoyed what they ordered, from egg pie to mocha roll and refreshing drinks.

There’s a cozy seating area for about 20 people, and even with our large group, the service was smooth and friendly.

Blacktown. September 2025. I've ordered a Cappuccino but it's not a Cappuccino.... it's Flat White   and just chocolate power on the top. Very disappointing Coffee. I didn't finish the coffee as the taste is bad. It made my day bad in the morning.

Such a wonderful cosy place. I originally went in to grab a coffee as i was just passing through and had no intention of grabbing any food. However, when i laid my eyes on their cakes, I couldnt help myself. I bought the most decadent chocolate cake slice which my husband and I fought over the last bite. It wasnt too sweet, it was soft and a big slice. Great customer service aswell.

name: Sunny Corner Cafe
address: Shop 1/2 Hill End Rd, Doonside NSW 2767, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
Don’t be fooled by its small size — Eskinita Café, right across from Doonside Station, is big in its offerings and flavours! We came as a party of seven (two families with three kids) and enjoyed a truly satisfying lunch.

The place felt cozy and welcoming, and our table was soon filled with delicious Filipino favourites. The kids devoured the barbecue, garlic rice, crispy pata, and daing na bangus — every dish cooked beautifully. My husband and I especially loved the sisig, pancit, and perfectly cooked chopsuey with fresh, crunchy vegetables.

And of course, no meal is complete without dessert — the kids were thrilled with their generously sized halo-halo, which was as refreshing as it was delicious! Service is topnotch, too - highly recommended.

A level up from X-Eskinita. If you are craving for authentic Lomi and Lugaw, this is the perfect place for you. Such a humble place, and I never felt away from home. Top-notch flavours, servings, and service are impeccable. Kudos to the new owner for bringing these real flavors here. It is a dog-friendly 🐶 and coffee is very good too. ☕️

X-Eskinita, now called Sunny Corner Cafe under new owner/manager. Food is mouth watering and how it looks is  also how it taste - superbly yummilicious! Serving is generous! Its big which I thought I can't finish but I did! "Simot-Sarap"!! Very friendly new owner/manager too!
We will come back for the silog & coffee, we heard a lot of good reviews on this too!
Their tapsilog and tocilog taste good. Some spiced vinegar would have made it perfect. Their halo halo was yummy and a really huge serving even though the sago is missing.

Amazing little place right beside the Train station in Doonside, I ordered a Tomato and cheese roll with caramelised onion in a roll and it blew me away the simplicity of it but the flavours were beautiful and it was filling, great place to get a snack if your walking round the area!

address: Carlisle Ave, Mount Druitt NSW 2770, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews: Came on a Sunday arvo and randomly selected breads including suggestions by the staff. I realise now we should've tried the pork buns but didn't get the chance. The ones we tried weren't particularly good. There was a strong taste of margarine in most of the filling. Many of the breads also had a shade of margarine yellow. But not the pandesal: theirs is made of white flour and on the sweet side, only lightly dipped in breadcrumbs with barely a noticeable "sal" texture. What we will remember and consider returning for is the energy and eagerness of the staff.
Great Filipino bakeshop with some decent bread. Price is fair and the pandesal was very soft. The ensaymada taste good as well.

I just walked past and smelled the nice bread and beautiful music. Decided to walk in. The price of each piece of bread is 1-3$. So I just tried some of them. Everything is so good. I like Butchi (sesame ball), caramel cake, and egg pie. Despite the shop looking old and unattractive, the quality of the food is amazing. Highly recommend.

Absolutely love this bakery I decided to try it because I moved schools and it was at the station I had to wait at for the bus and it just smelt so good so I decided to try it the pork buns are my personal favourite everyday they’re out I get one and I’ve slowly been trying the other stuff as well and all of it I’ve liked I go here multiple times a week when I have money 😻
Best pan-de-sal (bread with salt, but don't let the name fool you - it's a deliciously sweet, yeast risen bread) in Sydney. I bought 5 dozen for 2 adults and 2 kids, and they didn't last us a week!

Taguan Cafe
address: 274 Abercrombie St, Darlington NSW 2008, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
I got up early to find this place because I was on a hunt for pandesal. Thankfully, they had just baked some fresh ones! I was welcomed by my fellow kabayan mafias and bless their hearts for serving me a fresh brewed coffee from PH. Their cafe is perfect for slow mornings! Come all and come through!

P.S: they’ve got stickers from Magenta house 🥹
Very good Filipino dishes served in this cafe. Albeit we had to wait a while since they run out of beef tapa, chicken tocino and rice! But when they were served, we enjoyed them - tasty.

Literally my favourite café
There are a lot of really great cafes in our lovely Sydney, but nothing quite gets close to this Taguan.

The staff are the most lovely people you could possibly imagine meeting in a cafe that feels more like your family home than a business, filled with so much warmth thanks to all their smiling faces.

Sometimes when you show up they’ve got a new fun drink or food item that you’ve never heard of before, you should definitely give it a go if it sounds even remotely appealing to you. Sometimes it’ll be good, sometimes it’ll be absolutely mind-bogglingly amazing and delicious.

You can’t go wrong with any of their coffees, seriously like just order anything on the menu and you will have a good coffee

I’m a huge fan of the indulgent Ube grilled cheese, but honestly, I haven’t had any food at this place that isn’t handsdown great. Presentation, and taste.

I wish I had time to go here more often, and any time I have the chance to invite someone to come here with me I do.

Everything about this cafe is wonderful! The food is delicious, and the atmosphere is inviting. The service is second to none, regardless of whether you are quickly stopping to grab a coffee or dining alone, they are attentive to your needs.

I won’t pay for over priced or overhyped food, so the fact that I eat here regularly says a lot. They do justice to Filipino food, and I really do get have my cravings satisfied whenever I miss my Mums food.
Our fave cafe in Sydney! Filipino food done CORRECT. Great atmosphere, great food, great service. Highly recommend coming here to get a taste of Filipino food and hospitality

name: Takam, Filipino Food Forward
address: 324 Victoria St, Darlinghurst NSW 2010, Australia
description: A verified Filipino restaurant in Sydney.
reviews:
We tried the $98 per person set menu at Takam and it was absolutely worth it. The whole dining experience felt like a well-thought-out journey, with each course flowing nicely into the next. Every dish was beautifully presented and full of flavour, showing a lot of creativity and attention to detail.

The atmosphere was warm and inviting, making it perfect for a relaxed dinner or a special occasion. The staff were friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable, and they made us feel genuinely welcomed throughout the night.

Overall, great food, great service, and great value for money. Highly recommended and definitely keen to come back.

Came to this Filipino restaurant for lunch and it did not disappoint. Staff were very friendly and gave us detailed information on their menu. Calamansi soda and sarsaparilla were both very nice. The next table at a ube thick shake which was very tempting. Started off with the calamari which had nice sour notes to awaken the palate. This was followed by the swordfish which was excellent. Mains of beef, Murray cod with their version of sambal and the chicken were all very good. The crab fat fried rice had the expected umami hit. Leche flan was nice but we were quite full so could not order more dessert. A nice restaurant overall.

An Unforgettable Birthday Celebration at Takam!
We recently celebrated my daughter's birthday at Takam, from the moment we walked in, the warmth of the hospitality was incredible. Takam's friendly and attentive staff made us feel like family from start to finish.
A massive thank you to Patrick and the rest of the floor team; they were so professional and went above and beyond to make the celebration extra special. The service was impeccable-attentive without ever being intrusive.
The food was absolutely delicious! The chefs at Takam are masters of their craft, taking traditional Filipino flavours and giving them a refined, modern twist. The lemongrass chicken inasal was perfectly charred, and the Byron Bay_pork cutlet was a total standout. We loved the Palabok it had the perfect balance of rich shrimp sauce& to finish it off the Leche Flan was delightful.
If you are looking for a place with superb service and top-tier Filipino food for any occasion, look no further than Takam. We can't wait to come back. Maraming Salamat Po

Absolutely 5 stars! Love seeing Filipino food being presented the right way — beautifully cooked, thoughtful, and full of authentic flavour. The service was warm and attentive, and the whole experience felt so welcoming. Takam Sydney truly showcases Filipino cuisine with pride. Highly recommend!

I came to Takam for my birthday lunch with family. I had such a lovely experience & couldn’t recommend them enough. The service was amazing & the food even better. Patrick and the lovely team of talented chefs took great care of us. 5 stars ⭐️
